params.name: Rameau's Nephew
params.description:
On Jun 26, 1805 AD, Goethe translated "Rameau's Nephew" into German.
It is a philosophical novel by French encyclopedist Denis Diderot. It was not published during Diderot's lifetime. The novel, written in dialogue form, reveals the corruption of high society through the shameless speech of Rameau's nephew and offers unique insights into moral and artistic issues. The book breaks the boundaries of traditional tragedy and comedy, creating a new genre of serious drama, which Karl Marx considere …
